candidate_check.description: RHEL 9 user account passwords for new users or password changes must have a 60-day maximum password lifetime restriction in /etc/login.defs.

check_content_excerpt:
Verify that RHEL 9 enforces a 60-day maximum password lifetime for new user accounts by running the following command:

$ grep -i pass_max_days /etc/login.defs

PASS_MAX_DAYS 60

If the "PASS_MAX_DAYS" parameter value is greater than "60", or commented out, this is a finding.
fix_text_excerpt:
Configure RHEL 9 to enforce a 60-day maximum password lifetime.

Add or modify the following line in the "/etc/login.defs" file:

PASS_MAX_DAYS 60
title: RHEL 9 user account passwords for new users or password changes must have a 60-day maximum password lifetime restriction in /etc/login.defs.
